documentation.text:
@dev Contract module which provides a basic access control mechanism, where
 there is an account (an owner) that can be granted exclusive access to
 specific functions.
 The initial owner is set to the address provided by the deployer. This can
 later be changed with {transferOwnership}.
 This module is used through inheritance. It will make available the modifier
 `onlyOwner`, which can be applied to your functions to restrict their use to
 the owner.
@dev The caller account is not authorized to perform an operation.
@dev The owner is not a valid owner account. (eg. `address(0)`)
@dev Initializes the contract setting the address provided by the deployer as the initial owner.
@dev Throws if called by any account other than the owner.
@dev Returns the address of the current owner.
@dev Throws if the sender is not the owner.

@dev Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call
 `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner.
 NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner,
 thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

@dev Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`).
 Can only be called by the current owner.

@dev Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`).
 Internal function without access restriction.

@dev Provides information about the current execution context, including the
 sender of the transaction and its data. While these are generally available
 via msg.sender and msg.data, they should not be accessed in such a direct
 manner, since when dealing with meta-transactions the account sending and
 paying for execution may not be the actual sender (as far as an application
 is concerned).
 This contract is only required for intermediate, library-like contracts.

@dev Contract module that helps prevent reentrant calls to a function.
 Inheriting from `ReentrancyGuard` will make the {nonReentrant} modifier
 available, which can be applied to functions to make sure there are no nested
 (reentrant) calls to them.
 Note that because there is a single `nonReentrant` guard, functions marked as
 `nonReentrant` may not call one another. This can be worked around by making
 those functions `private`, and then adding `external` `nonReentrant` entry
 points to them.
 TIP: If EIP-1153 (transient storage) is available on the chain you're deploying at,
 consider using {ReentrancyGuardTransient} instead.
 TIP: If you would like to learn more about reentrancy and alternative ways
 to protect against it, check out our blog post
 https://blog.openzeppelin.com/reentrancy-after-istanbul/[Reentrancy After Istanbul].
@dev Unauthorized reentrant call.

@dev Prevents a contract from calling itself, directly or indirectly.
 Calling a `nonReentrant` function from another `nonReentrant`
 function is not supported. It is possible to prevent this from happening
 by making the `nonReentrant` function external, and making it call a
 `private` function that does the actual work.

@dev Returns true if the reentrancy guard is currently set to "entered", which indicates there is a
 `nonReentrant` function in the call stack.

@title BountyMarket
 @author VAPI Project
 @notice Decentralized bounty marketplace for VAPI autonomous physical
         intelligence agents on IoTeX. Enables requesters to post bounties
         for real-world data collection, verified through Proof of Autonomous
         Cognition (PoAC) records.
 @dev Lifecycle: Create -> Active -> (Accepted -> Fulfilled | Expired)
      Bounty parameters mirror the firmware's `bounty_descriptor_t` from
      `economic.h`, with monetary values denominated in native IOTX (wei).
      Evidence is linked to verified PoAC records. The contract checks:
        - The device accepted this bounty
        - The PoAC is verified on-chain (via PoACVerifier)
        - Geographic zone containment
        - Sensor requirements met
        - Sample interval respected
      Swarm aggregation enables multiple devices to collaboratively report
      on overlapping physical events, producing a confidence-weighted
      PhysicalOracleReport suitable for consumption by other DeFi protocols.
@notice Minimum bounty duration in seconds.
@notice Maximum bounty duration in seconds.
@notice Coordinate scaling factor: firmware sends lat/lon * 1e7 as int64.
@notice Platform fee in basis points (e.g., 250 = 2.5%).
@notice Accumulated platform fees available for withdrawal.
@notice Bounty lifecycle states.
@notice Per-device acceptance record for a bounty.
@notice Evidence linking a PoAC record to a bounty.
@notice Swarm aggregation report — physical oracle output.
@notice References to companion contracts.
@notice Auto-incrementing bounty ID counter.
@notice bountyId => BountyDescriptor.
@notice bountyId => deviceId => Acceptance.
@notice bountyId => array of Evidence records.
@notice bountyId => set of deviceIds that have accepted.
@notice recordHash => bountyId (prevent double-submission of evidence).
@notice Emitted when a new bounty is posted.
@notice Emitted when a device accepts a bounty.
@notice Emitted when evidence is submitted linking a PoAC to a bounty.
@notice Emitted when a device claims the reward for a fulfilled bounty.
@notice Emitted when a bounty expires and the creator is refunded.
@notice Thrown when a device's registration tier does not permit bounty claims.

@notice Update the platform fee. Owner-only.
 @param _newFeeBps New fee in basis points (max 1000 = 10%).
@notice Withdraw accumulated platform fees. Owner-only.

@notice Minimum registration deposit in wei (anti-Sybil).

 @param _newMinimum New minimum deposit in wei.

@notice Withdraw deposit after deactivation cooldown has elapsed.
 @param _deviceId The deactivated device whose deposit to withdraw.

@dev Internal reputation computation.
      Formula (simple linear model with diminishing returns on volume):
        - verifiedBase  = min(verifiedCount, 10000) (cap raw PoAC contribution)
        - corrobBonus   = corroborations * 2
        - disputePenalty = disputes * 10
        - rawScore = verifiedBase + corrobBonus - disputePenalty
        - Normalize into [0, MAX_REPUTATION] using a logistic-style clamp:
          score = rawScore * MAX_REPUTATION / (rawScore + 1000)
          (Approaches MAX_REPUTATION asymptotically as rawScore grows)
        - If rawScore <= 0, score = 0.

@title PoACVerifier
 @author VAPI Project
 @notice On-chain verification of Proof of Autonomous Cognition (PoAC) records
         submitted by VAPI devices on the IoTeX blockchain.
 @dev Verification accepts the raw 164-byte body exactly as signed by the
      firmware (no re-serialization), plus the 64-byte ECDSA-P256 signature.
      Pipeline:
        1. Validate body/signature lengths (164 / 64 bytes).
        2. Check device is registered and active in {DeviceRegistry}.
        3. Compute SHA-256 of the raw body (identical to firmware digest).
        4. Verify the ECDSA-P256 signature over that SHA-256 digest
           using the IoTeX P256 precompile at address 0x0100.
        5. Parse fields from the raw body for semantic validation.
        6. Validate monotonic counter, timestamp skew, chain linkage.
        7. Store SHA-256(body) as the chain head and record hash.
           This matches the firmware's chain_head = SHA-256(serialized_body),
           ensuring prev_poac_hash linkage is consistent end-to-end.
        8. Update the device's reputation in {DeviceRegistry}.
      Raw body layout (big-endian, matching firmware poac_serialize()):
        Offset  Field                  Size
        0x00    prev_poac_hash         32B
        0x20    sensor_commitment      32B
        0x40    model_manifest_hash    32B
        0x60    world_model_hash       32B
        0x80    inference_result       1B
        0x81    action_code            1B
        0x82    confidence             1B
        0x83    battery_pct            1B
        0x84    monotonic_ctr          4B   (uint32 big-endian)
        0x88    timestamp_ms           8B   (int64 big-endian)
        0x90    latitude               8B   (IEEE 754 double big-endian)
        0x98    longitude              8B   (IEEE 754 double big-endian)
        0xA0    bounty_id              4B   (uint32 big-endian)
        ---     total                  164B
      ## IoTeX P256 Precompile (0x0100)
      Input:  hash(32) || r(32) || s(32) || x(32) || y(32) = 160 bytes
      Output: 0x01 (32-byte left-padded) if valid, 0x00 otherwise.
@notice IoTeX P256 signature verification precompile address.
@notice Size of the serialized PoAC body (before signature).
@notice Size of the ECDSA-P256 signature (r || s).
@notice Maximum allowable timestamp skew (seconds).
@notice Chain tracking state for each device.
@notice Reference to the DeviceRegistry contract.
@notice Per-device chain integrity state. deviceId => ChainState.
@notice Record hash => true if this PoAC has been verified on-chain.
@notice Total number of PoAC records verified across all devices.
@notice Emitted when a PoAC record is successfully verified.
@notice Emitted when a batch verification completes.

@param _deviceRegistry   Address of the deployed DeviceRegistry contract.
 @param _maxTimestampSkew  Maximum acceptable timestamp skew in seconds.
@notice External wrapper for try/catch in batch. Not for direct use.
@dev Parsed fields from the raw body, packed into a struct to avoid stack-too-deep.

typeDescriptions.typeString: function (bytes calldata) pure returns (struct PoACVerifier.ParsedFields memory)
function (bytes32) view external returns (struct DeviceRegistry.DeviceInfo memory)

documentation.text:
@title ProgressAttestation
 @author VAPI Project
 @notice Verifiable progress proofs for autonomous gaming coaching.
 @dev Stores cryptographic attestations of measurable skill improvement,
      backed by verified PoAC records. Coaching platforms can query these
      proofs to verify student improvement before issuing certificates,
      badges, or processing refund guarantees.
      Workflow:
        1. Student plays with VAPI device, generating PoAC chain
        2. Agent detects improvement (e.g., reaction_time decreased 15%)
        3. Coach or student calls attestProgress() with baseline + current records
        4. Contract verifies both records exist on-chain
        5. Progress attestation stored with metric type and improvement BPS
        6. Coaching platform queries getProgressHistory() for verification
      Metric types:
        REACTION_TIME   — Average reaction time improvement
        ACCURACY        — Stick/aim precision improvement
        CONSISTENCY     — Reduced timing variance
        COMBO_EXECUTION — Complex input sequence mastery
@notice Global attestation counter (also serves as attestation ID).
@notice attestationId => ProgressRecord
@notice deviceId => array of attestation IDs
@notice Prevent duplicate attestations for same baseline+current pair

typeDescriptions.typeString: function (uint256) pure returns (struct ProgressAttestation.ProgressRecord memory[] memory)

documentation.text:
@title SkillOracle
 @author VAPI Project
 @notice On-chain skill rating oracle for esports and gaming DAOs.
 @dev Aggregates verified PoAC records into an ELO-inspired skill rating
      per device. Gaming DAOs can query this oracle for:
        - Tournament eligibility gating
        - Governance weight by proven skill
        - Fair reward distribution based on performance
      Rating algorithm:
        - Base rating starts at 1000 (Bronze)
        - NOMINAL gameplay: +5 per verified record
        - SKILLED gameplay:  +12 per verified record
        - CHEAT detection:   -200 per cheat flag (harsh penalty)
        - Confidence scaling: gain *= confidence / 255
        - Rating floor: 0, ceiling: 3000
      Tier brackets:
        Bronze:   0 - 999
        Silver:   1000 - 1499
        Gold:     1500 - 1999
        Platinum: 2000 - 2499
        Diamond:  2500 - 3000
      Only processes records already verified by {PoACVerifier}.

@notice Minimum blocks between successive rating updates for the same device.
 @dev    Owner-adjustable. Set to 0 to disable rate limiting.
@notice Last block number at which a device received a rating update.

@title TeamProofAggregator
 @author VAPI Project
 @notice Swarm-based collaborative gaming with verifiable team proofs.
 @dev Aggregates individual PoAC record hashes from team members into a
      compact Merkle root that serves as a team-level attestation.
      Use cases:
        - Esports tournament integrity: all team members verified clean
        - Cooperative game achievements: verifiable team contribution
        - DAO governance: team-weighted voting based on collective proof
      Merkle construction:
        1. Sort individual record hashes lexicographically
        2. Compute pairwise keccak256 hashes up the tree
        3. Odd leaf is promoted unchanged
        4. Root = team attestation hash
      Integrity guarantees:
        - Every member's record must be verified in PoACVerifier
        - No member can have cheat flags (inference in [0x22, 0x29])
        - All members must belong to the registered team
@notice Global proof counter (also serves as proof ID).
@notice proofId => TeamProof
@notice teamId => array of proof IDs

@dev Compute Merkle root from an array of leaf hashes.
      Sorts leaves lexicographically, then hashes pairwise.
@dev Insertion sort for small arrays (max 6 elements).
@notice Verify a Merkle root against individual record hashes (off-chain helper).

@title TieredDeviceRegistry
 @author VAPI Project
 @notice Extends DeviceRegistry with a three-tier registration model for Sybil resistance.
 @dev IS-A DeviceRegistry — all downstream contracts (PoACVerifier, BountyMarket,
      SkillOracle) accept TieredDeviceRegistry through the DeviceRegistry interface
      without modification.
 Tier model (testnet / mainnet targets):
   Emulated  — DualShock/software  — 0.1 / 10 IOTX  — SkillOracle only, no bounty rewards
   Standard  — IoT devices         — 1   / 100 IOTX  — Full access, 50% reward weight
   Attested  — Pebble ioID cert    — 0.01 / 1 IOTX   — Full access, 100% reward weight
 Phase 8 work items (deferred):
   - Full ioID P256 certificate chain verification via IoTeX precompile 0x0100
   - BountyMarket / SkillOracle enforcement of tier capabilities
   - Certificate revocation mechanism
@notice Tier configuration per registration tier.
@notice Registration tier per device ID.
@notice Addresses of approved hardware manufacturers (for Attested tier).

@notice When false (testnet): any 64-byte proof accepted for Attested tier.
         When true (mainnet):  full ioID P256 cert verification required (Phase 8).
@notice P256 public key registry for approved hardware manufacturers.
@notice Emitted when a hardware attestation certificate hash is stored.
@notice Thrown when a caller tries to use registerTieredDevice for Attested tier.
@notice Thrown when a non-owner tries to update a device's cert hash.
@notice Thrown when msg.value < tier's required deposit.
@notice Thrown when attestation proof format is invalid (must be 64 bytes).
@notice Thrown when attestationEnforced=true but Phase 8 crypto is not yet implemented.
@notice Thrown when manufacturer address has no registered P256 key.
@notice Thrown when manufacturer's key has been revoked.
@notice Thrown when IoTeX P256 precompile rejects the attestation signature.

@dev V2 attestation validation using registered manufacturer P256 key.
      When attestationEnforced=false, skips crypto verification (testnet mode).
      When attestationEnforced=true, calls _p256Verify() which invokes the
      IoTeX P256 precompile at 0x0100. Override _p256Verify() in test harnesses.

@dev Calls IoTeX P256 precompile 0x0100 with 160-byte input:
      msgHash(32) || r(32) || s(32) || manuf_x(32) || manuf_y(32).
      Returns true iff the call succeeds and returns uint256(1).
      Virtual so test harnesses can override without calling the precompile.
@notice Get the registration tier of a device.

@notice Enable or disable strict attestation enforcement. Owner-only.
 @dev Set to true before mainnet launch when Phase 8 crypto is implemented.

@title TieredDeviceRegistryV2Testable
 @dev Test helper that overrides _p256Verify() so V2 attestation tests can
      exercise manufacturer key lookup and revocation logic without calling
      the IoTeX P256 precompile (0x0100), which is unavailable on Hardhat EVM.
      _validateAttestationV2() still enforces:
        - 64-byte proof length
        - attestationEnforced flag
        - NoApprovedManufacturer (key must be registered)
        - ManufacturerKeyInactive (key must be active)
      Only the final precompile call is bypassed — it always returns true.
      Set _mockP256Result = false to simulate precompile rejection (AttestationVerificationFailed).
@notice Owner can flip the mock P256 result for negative-path testing.

Project","details":"Each device is identified by a `deviceId` derived as keccak256 of its      uncompressed SEC1 P256 public key (65 bytes: 0x04 || x || y).      IoTeX supports ECDSA-P256 signature verification via a precompile at      address 0x0100. The EVM does not natively support P256, so all on-chain      signature checks MUST use this precompile. See {PoACVerifier} for usage.      Registration requires a deposit to prevent Sybil attacks. The deposit is      held for the lifetime of the device registration and can be reclaimed      upon deactivation after a cooldown period. dRecords","outputs":[{"internalType":"bool","name":"","type":"bool"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"_deviceId","type":"bytes32"},{"internalType":"bytes","name":"_rawBody","type":"bytes"},{"internalType":"bytes","name":"_signature","type":"bytes"}],"name":"verifyPoAC","outputs":[{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"recordHash","type":"bytes32"}],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes32[]","name":"_deviceIds","type":"bytes32[]"},{"internalType":"bytes[]","name":"_rawBodies","type":"bytes[]"},{"internalType":"bytes[]","name":"_signatures","type":"bytes[]"}],"name":"verifyPoACBatch","outputs":[{"internalType":"bytes32[]","name":"recordHashes","type":"bytes32[]"}],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"_deviceId","type":"bytes32"},{"internalType":"bytes","name":"_rawBody","type":"bytes"},{"internalType":"bytes","name":"_signature","type":"bytes"}],"name":"verifyPoACExternal","outputs":[{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"","type":"bytes32"}],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"author":"VAPI Project","details":"Verification accepts the raw 164-byte body exactly as signed by the      firmware (no re-serialization), plus the 64-byte ECDSA-P256 signature.      Pipeline:        1. Validate body/signature lengths (164 / 64 bytes).        2. Check device is registered and active in {DeviceRegistry}.        3. Compute SHA-256 of the raw body (identical to firmware digest).        4. Verify the ECDSA-P256 signature over that SHA-256 digest           using the IoTeX P256 precompile at address 0x0100.        5. Parse fields from the raw body for semantic validation.        6. Validate monotonic counter, timestamp skew, chain linkage.        7. Store SHA-256(body) as the chain head and record hash.           This matches the firmware's chain_head = SHA-256(serialized_body),           ensuring prev_poac_hash linkage is consistent end-to-end.        8. Update the device's reputation in {DeviceRegistry}.      Raw body layout (big-endian, matching firmware poac_serialize()):        Offset  Field                  Size        0x00    prev_poac_hash         32B        0x20    sensor_commitment      32B        0x40    model_manifest_hash    32B        0x60    world_model_hash       32B        0x80    inference_result       1B        0x81    action_code            1B        0x82    confidence             1B        0x83    battery_pct            1B        0x84    monotonic_ctr          4B   (uint32 big-endian)        0x88    timestamp_ms           8B   (int64 big-endian)        0x90    latitude               8B   (IEEE 754 double big-endian)        0x98    longitude              8B   (IEEE 754 double big-endian)        0xA0    bounty_id              4B   (uint32 big-endian)        ---     total                  164B      ## IoTeX P256 Precompile (0x0100)      Input:  hash(32) || r(32) || s(32) || x(32) || y(32) = 160 bytes      Output: 0x01 (32-byte left-padded) if valid, 0x00 otherwise.","errors":{"OwnableInvalidOwner(address)":[{"details":"The owner is not a valid owner account. (eg. `address(0)`)"}],"OwnableUnauthorizedAccount(address)":[{"details":"The caller account is not authorized to perform an operation."}],"ReentrancyGuardReentrantCall()":[{"details":"Unauthorized reentrant call."}]},"kind":"dev","methods":{"constructor":{"params":{"_deviceRegistry":"Address of the deployed DeviceRegistry contract.","_maxTimestampSkew":"Maximum acceptable timestamp skew in seconds."}},"owner()":{"details":"Returns the address of the current owner."},"renounceOwnership()":{"details":"Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner."},"transferOwnership(address)":{"details":"Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner."},"verifyPoAC(bytes32,bytes,bytes)":{"params":{"_deviceId":"Device identifier (keccak256 of device pubkey).","_rawBody":"The 164-byte serialized PoAC body (exactly as signed).","_signature":"ECDSA-P256 signature (r || s, 64 bytes)."},"returns":{"recordHash":"SHA-256 of the body (matches firmware chain head)."}},"verifyPoACBatch(bytes32[],bytes[],bytes[])":{"details":"Records that fail are silently skipped (not reverted).      The BatchVerified event reports counts."}},"stateVariables":{"submittedHashes":{"details":"Unlike {verifiedRecords} (set only on full verification success),         submittedHashes is set in the OUTER batch context so it persists even         when the child verification call reverts, permanently preventing         re-submission of any body that entered a batch \u2014 regardless of outcome."}},"title":"PoACVerifier","version":1},"userdoc":{"events":{"BatchVerified(uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when a batch verification completes."},"PoACVerified(bytes32,bytes32,uint32,int64,uint8,uint8,uint32)":{"notice":"Emitted when a PoAC record is successfully verified."}},"kind":"user","methods":{"P256_PRECOMPILE()":{"notice":"IoTeX P256 signature verification precompile address."},"POAC_BODY_SIZE()":{"notice":"Size of the serialized PoAC body (before signature)."},"POAC_SIG_SIZE()":{"notice":"Size of the ECDSA-P256 signature (r || s)."},"deviceRegistry()":{"notice":"Reference to the DeviceRegistry contract."},"maxTimestampSkew()":{"notice":"Maximum allowable timestamp skew (seconds)."},"submittedHashes(bytes32)":{"notice":"Record hash => true if this body was ever submitted to this contract."},"totalVerifiedCount()":{"notice":"Total number of PoAC records verified across all devices."},"verifiedRecords(bytes32)":{"notice":"Record hash => true if this PoAC has been verified on-chain."},"verifyPoAC(bytes32,bytes,bytes)":{"notice":"Verify a single PoAC record from its raw wire-format bytes."},"verifyPoACBatch(bytes32[],bytes[],bytes[])":{"notice":"Batch-verify multiple PoAC records."},"verifyPoACExternal(bytes32,bytes,bytes)":{"notice":"External wrapper for try/catch in batch. Not for direct use."}},"notice":"On-chain verification of Proof of Autonomous Cognition (PoAC) records         submitted by VAPI devices on the IoTeX blockchain.","version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"contracts/PoACVerifier.sol":"PoACVerifier"},"evmVersion":"paris","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":true,"runs":200},"remappings":[],"viaIR":true},"sources":{"@openzeppelin/contracts/access/Ownable.sol":{"keccak256":"0xff6d0bb2e285473e5311d9d3caacb525ae3538a80758c10649a4d61029b017bb","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://8ed324d3920bb545059d66ab97d43e43ee85fd3bd52e03e401f020afb0b120f6","dweb:/ipfs/QmfEckWLmZkDDcoWrkEvMWhms66xwTLff9DDhegYpvHo1a"]},"@openzeppelin/contracts/utils/Context.sol":{"keccak256":"0x493033a8d1b176a037b2cc6a04dad01a5c157722049bbecf632ca876224dd4b2","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://6a708e8a5bdb1011c2c381c9a5cfd8a9a956d7d0a9dc1bd8bcdaf52f76ef2f12","dweb:/ipfs/Qmax9WHBnVsZP46ZxEMNRQpLQnrdE4dK8LehML1Py8FowF"]},"@openzeppelin/contracts/utils/ReentrancyGuard.sol":{"keccak256":"0x11a5a79827df29e915a12740caf62fe21ebe27c08c9ae3e09abe9ee3ba3866d3","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://3cf0c69ab827e3251db9ee6a50647d62c90ba580a4d7bbff21f2bea39e7b2f4a","dweb:/ipfs/QmZiKwtKU1SBX4RGfQtY7PZfiapbbu6SZ9vizGQD9UHjRA"]},"contracts/DeviceRegistry.sol":{"keccak256":"0x3794c85edafd0283c0f794c64dccba867675d1c59ef22a1e190129becfd75395","license":"Apache-2.0","urls":["bzz-raw://84823e48467ec6ec1ab3c5b67a27038401423a7c6030e9349b7ed27a0556a8b3","dweb:/ipfs/QmUtDEVaNJ9J8mDT8TVMkLFwWQHGCiLHnGWD6SH3sFeBvY"]},"contracts/PoACVerifier.sol":{"keccak256":"0x39858ba94cc7ed48842378da364ae9e3915a8688b91cb576838b00a0dc1d3fed","license":"Apache-2.0","urls":["bzz-raw://7c783ba07af79a2d502d1419aba174991d5c142605aedc8174f98f5fd9d21d0b","dweb:/ipfs/QmT5DhfNDQ55yHxaHxdGQ8A91eFBXf2kev73Hx4GrNZzDV"]}},"version":1}

Project","details":"Stores cryptographic attestations of measurable skill improvement,      backed by verified PoAC records. Coaching platforms can query these      proofs to verify student improvement before issuing certificates,      badges, or processing refund guarantees.      Workflow:        1. Student plays with VAPI device, generating PoAC chain        2. Agent detects improvement (e.g., reaction_time decreased 15%)        3. Coach or student calls attestProgress() with baseline + current records        4. Contract verifies both records exist on-chain        5. Progress attestation stored with metric type and improvement BPS        6. Coaching platform queries getProgressHistory() for verification      Metric types:        REACTION_TIME   \u2014 Average reaction time improvement        ACCURACY        \u2014 Stick/aim precision improvement        CONSISTENCY     \u2014 Reduced timing variance        COMBO_EXECUTION \u2014 Complex input sequence mastery","errors":{"OwnableInvalidOwner(address)":[{"details":"The owner is not a valid owner account. (eg. `address(0)`)"}],"OwnableUnauthorizedAccount(address)":[{"details":"The caller account is not authorized to perform an operation."}]},"kind":"dev","methods":{"attestProgress(bytes32,bytes32,bytes32,uint8,uint32)":{"params":{"_baselineHash":"SHA-256 hash of the baseline (pre-coaching) PoAC body.","_currentHash":"SHA-256 hash of the current (post-coaching) PoAC body.","_deviceId":"Device that generated both records.","_improvementBps":"Improvement magnitude in basis points (100 = 1%).","_metricType":"Which skill metric improved."},"returns":{"attestationId":"   Unique ID for this attestation."}},"owner()":{"details":"Returns the address of the current owner."},"renounceOwnership()":{"details":"Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner."},"transferOwnership(address)":{"details":"Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner."}},"title":"ProgressAttestation","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"attestProgress(bytes32,bytes32,bytes32,uint8,uint32)":{"notice":"Attest to autonomous gaming Project","details":"Aggregates verified PoAC records into an ELO-inspired skill rating      per device. Gaming DAOs can query this oracle for:        - Tournament eligibility gating        - Governance weight by proven skill        - Fair reward distribution based on performance      Rating algorithm:        - Base rating starts at 1000 (Bronze)        - NOMINAL gameplay: +5 per verified record        - SKILLED gameplay:  +12 per verified record        - CHEAT detection:   -200 per cheat flag (harsh penalty)        - Confidence scaling: gain *= confidence / 255        - Rating floor: 0, ceiling: 3000      Tier brackets:        Bronze:   0 - 999        Silver:   1000 - 1499        Gold:     1500 - 1999        Platinum: 2000 - 2499        Diamond:  2500 - 3000      Only processes records already verified by {PoACVerifier}.","errors":{"OwnableInvalidOwner(address)":[{"details":"The owner is not a valid owner account. (eg. `address(0)`)"}],"OwnableUnauthorizedAccount(address)":[{"details":"The caller account is not authorized to perform an operation."}]},"kind":"dev","methods":{"owner()":{"details":"Returns the address of the current owner."},"renounceOwnership()":{"details":"Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner."},"setMinUpdateInterval(uint256)":{"details":"Set to 0 to disable rate limiting entirely.         At IoTeX's ~5s block time, 12 blocks \u2248 60s minimum between updates."},"transferOwnership(address)":{"details":"Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner."},"updateSkillRating(bytes32,bytes32,uint8,uint8)":{"params":{"_confidence":"The confidence value [0-255] from the PoAC record.","_deviceId":"Device identifier.","_inferenceResult":"The inference code from the PoAC record.","_recordHash":"SHA-256 hash of the verified PoAC body."}}},"stateVariables":{"DEFAULT_MIN_INTERVAL":{"details":"At IoTeX's ~5s block time, 1 block \u2248 5 seconds between updates.         Prevents same-block burst manipulation (submitting all historical         records in one block to spike rating for tournament eligibility)."},"minUpdateInterval":{"details":"Owner-adjustable. Set to 0 to disable rate limiting."}},"title":"SkillOracle","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"DEFAULT_MIN_INTERVAL()":{"notice":"Default minimum block interval between rating updates per device."},"minUpdateInterval()":{"notice":"Minimum blocks between successive rating updates for the same device."},"setMinUpdateInterval(uint256)":{"notice":"Set the minimum block interval between Project","details":"Aggregates individual PoAC record hashes from team members into a      compact Merkle root that serves as a team-level attestation.      Use cases:        - Esports tournament integrity: all team members verified clean        - Cooperative game achievements: verifiable team contribution        - DAO governance: team-weighted voting based on collective proof      Merkle construction:        1. Sort individual record hashes lexicographically        2. Compute pairwise keccak256 hashes up the tree        3. Odd leaf is promoted unchanged        4. Root = team attestation hash      Integrity guarantees:        - Every member's record must be verified in PoACVerifier        - No member can have cheat flags (inference in [0x22, 0x29])        - All members must belong to the registered team","errors":{"OwnableInvalidOwner(address)":[{"details":"The owner is not a valid owner account. (eg. `address(0)`)"}],"OwnableUnauthorizedAccount(address)":[{"details":"The caller account is not authorized to perform an operation."}]},"kind":"dev","methods":{"createTeam(bytes32,bytes32[])":{"params":{"_deviceIds":"Array of member device IDs (2-6 members).","_teamId":"Unique team identifier (e.g., keccak256 of team name)."}},"owner()":{"details":"Returns the address of the current owner."},"renounceOwnership()":{"details":"Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner."},"submitTeamProof(bytes32,bytes32[],bytes32)":{"params":{"_merkleRoot":"Pre-computed Merkle root of sorted record hashes.","_recordHashes":"One verified PoAC record hash per team member (in member order).","_teamId":"Team identifier."},"returns":{"proofId":"      Unique proof identifier."}},"transferOwnership(address)":{"details":"Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). ManufacturerKeyInactive (key must be active)      Only the final precompile call is bypassed \u2014 it always returns true.
